title: 5. Nothing Is Over | Do We Get to Win This Time?
format: The Vietnam-on-film series traces how First Blood turned a doomed-vet novel into a franchise, and how the POW-rescue movies let Reagan-era audiences re-fight the war.

segments.heading: How Hollywood Made the Vietnam War
segments.note: First Blood and the Vetsploitation wave

films.title: The Deer Hunter
films.note: The 1979 screening that shook vet Jan Scruggs and set him on the path to the Vietnam memorial wall.

films.title: First Blood
films.note: David Morrell's novel adapted for the screen by Kotcheff and Stallone, who flipped the ending so Rambo survives instead of dying with Teasle as in the book.

films.title: North Dallas Forty
films.note: Cited as one end of director Ted Kotcheff's range before First Blood.

films.title: Fun with Dick and Jane
films.note: The Jane Fonda caper, cited as the other end of Kotcheff's filmography.

films.title: Uncommon Valor
films.note: Kotcheff's follow-up; Gene Hackman leads a POW-rescue mission. Paramount recut the marketing to sell it as feel-good after fans said they liked it because 'we get to win the Vietnam War.'

films.title: Missing in Action
films.note: Chuck Norris as Braddock returning to free POWs; Norris's brother Wieland was killed in Vietnam in 1970.

films.title: Rambo: First Blood Part II
films.note: James Cameron drafted the script, Stallone revised it; Julia Nixon plays Co. A number-one hit that Reagan quoted and that vets picketed as pro-war propaganda.

films.title: Cobra
films.note: Cited as director George P. Cosmatos's later collaboration with Stallone.

films.title: Strike Commando
films.note: Named as one of the Vetsploitation knockoffs that filled 80s video-store shelves.

films.title: Deadly Prey
films.note: Another Vetsploitation title cited in the same breath as Strike Commando.

Ads: Netflix FIFA World Cup Launch Edition
